alabama.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance and OSHA training obligations still apply.

alaska.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
alaska.other: Alaska operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (AKOSH) covering most private-sector workers, with its own documented safety-training obligations.

arizona.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
arizona.other: Arizona operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (ADOSH) with its own safety-training and heat-illness-prevention requirements.

arkansas.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

california.harassDetail: Employers with 5 or more employees must provide 2 hours of interactive sexual-harassment-prevention training to supervisors and 1 hour to non-supervisory employees every 2 years, and within 6 months of hire or promotion.
california.other: State-plan OSHA (Cal/OSHA) requires documented workplace safety training, including under the Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP).

colorado.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; the POWR Act (2023) lowered the standard for harassment claims and encourages training as part of an employer defense, but training itself is not required. Federal EEOC guidance still applies.

connecticut.harassDetail: Employers with 3 or more employees must provide 2 hours of sexual-harassment-prevention training to all employees within 6 months of hire, with supplemental training at least every 10 years.

delaware.harassDetail: Employers with 50 or more employees in the state must provide interactive sexual-harassment-prevention training to all employees within 1 year of hire and every 2 years thereafter; smaller employers are encouraged but not required to train.
delaware.harassLaw: Delaware Discrimination in Employment Act, 19 Del. C. Ch. 7

florida.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

georgia.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

hawaii.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
hawaii.other: Hawaii operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (HIOSH) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

idaho.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

illinois.harassDetail: All employers with employees working in Illinois must provide annual interactive sexual-harassment-prevention training to every employee, using the state model program or an equivalent that meets Illinois Human Rights Act minimum standards; restaurants and bars face additional supplemental training rules.
illinois.other: Chicago separately requires 1 hour of annual harassment-prevention training for all employees plus 2 hours for supervisors, and 1 hour of bystander-intervention training, each year.

indiana.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
indiana.other: Indiana operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (IOSHA) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

iowa.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
iowa.other: Iowa operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (Iowa OSHA) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

kansas.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

kentucky.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
kentucky.other: Kentucky operates an OSHA-approved State Plan with its own documented safety-training requirements.

louisiana.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

maine.harassDetail: Employers with 15 or more employees must provide sexual-harassment-prevention training to all employees within 1 year of hire, with additional supervisor-specific training within 1 year of taking on supervisory duties; annual written notice to employees is also required.

maryland.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
maryland.other: Maryland operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (MOSH) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

massachusetts.harassDetail: No statewide mandatory sexual-harassment-training law for private employers today; the state requires a written policy and strongly recommends training, but training itself is not mandated. Federal EEOC guidance still applies.

michigan.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
michigan.other: Michigan operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (MIOSHA) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

minnesota.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
minnesota.other: Minnesota operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (MNOSHA) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

mississippi.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

missouri.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

montana.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

nebraska.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

nevada.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today (public-sector employees have a separate requirement); federal EEOC guidance still applies to private employers, including hospitality and gaming.
nevada.other: Nevada operates an OSHA-approved State Plan with its own documented safety-training requirements relevant to hospitality and gaming.

new-hampshire.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

new-jersey.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

new-mexico.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
new-mexico.other: New Mexico operates an OSHA-approved State Plan with its own documented safety-training requirements.

new-york.harassDetail: Every employer, regardless of size, must adopt a sexual-harassment-prevention policy and provide interactive training to all employees annually, using the state model or an equivalent program. New York City employers with 15 or more employees face additional annual training requirements.
new-york.other: New York City's Human Rights Law separately requires covered employers to provide annual interactive harassment-prevention training to employees and interns working in NYC.

north-carolina.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
north-carolina.other: North Carolina operates an OSHA-approved State Plan with its own documented safety-training requirements.

north-dakota.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

ohio.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

oklahoma.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

oregon.harassDetail: No statewide mandatory sexual-harassment-training law for private employers today; the Workplace Fairness Act requires a written anti-harassment policy and training is encouraged, but not required. Federal EEOC guidance still applies.
oregon.other: Oregon operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (Oregon OSHA) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

pennsylvania.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

rhode-island.harassDetail: The status is nuanced: Rhode Island requires employers with 50 or more employees to adopt a harassment policy and to train supervisory employees, but does not mandate training for all employees statewide. Verify current statute language before treating this as a full mandate. Federal EEOC guidance also applies.
rhode-island.harassLaw: R.I. Gen. Laws Section 28-51-1 et seq.

south-carolina.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
south-carolina.other: South Carolina operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (SC OSHA) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

south-dakota.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

tennessee.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
tennessee.other: Tennessee operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (TOSHA) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

texas.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

utah.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
utah.other: Utah operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (UOSH) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

vermont.harassDetail: No statewide mandatory sexual-harassment-training law for private employers today; all employers must have a written anti-harassment policy and training is encouraged, but not required unless ordered after a compliance issue. Federal EEOC guidance still applies.
vermont.other: Vermont operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (VOSHA) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

virginia.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
virginia.other: Virginia operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (VOSH) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

washington.harassDetail: Employers in specified industries (hotel, motel, retail, security-guard, and property-services contractors) must provide harassment-and-assault-prevention training to isolated workers and, effective January 1, 2026, to managers and supervisors at those employers; this is a targeted industry mandate, not a general requirement for all private employers.
washington.other: Washington operates an OSHA-approved State Plan (WISHA / L&I) with its own documented safety-training requirements.

west-virginia.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

wisconsin.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.

wyoming.harassDetail: No statewide sexual-harassment-training mandate for private employers today; federal EEOC guidance still applies.
wyoming.other: Wyoming operates an OSHA-approved State Plan with its own documented safety-training requirements.
